content.problemStatement:
You are given a tree with NNN vertices (numbered 111 through NNN). Its edges are numbered 111 through N−1N-1N−1. For each valid iii, there is an integer aia_iai​ written on the iii-th vertex. Also, for each valid iii, there is an integer bib_ibi​ written on the iii-th edge.

You want the following condition to be satisfied: for each vertex vvv and each edge eee adjacent to vvv, av≥bea_v \ge b_eav​≥be​. In order to achieve that, you may perform an arbitrary number of steps (including zero). In one step, you may perform one the following operations:


Select two different vertices uuu and vvv. Swap aua_uau​ and ava_vav​.
Select two different edges eee and fff. Swap beb_ebe​ and bfb_fbf​.
Select a vertex vvv and an integer xxx. Change ava_vav​ to xxx and pay one coin.
Calculate the minimum number of coins you need in order to satisfy the required condition.
constraints: 1≤ui,vi≤N1 \le u_i, v_i \le N1≤ui​,vi​≤N for each valid iii
the graph on the input is a tree
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
N−1N-1N−1 lines follow. For each iii (1≤i≤N−11 \le i \le N-11≤i≤N−1), the iii-th of these lines contains three space-separated integers uiu_iui​, viv_ivi​ and bib_ibi​ denoting that the iii-th edge connects vertices uiu_iui​ and viv_ivi​ and the initial value written on it is bib_ibi​.
The last line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,…,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1​,a2​,…,aN​.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the minimum necessary number of coins.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There is no way to satisfy the required condition without paying any coins. When we have one coin, we can perform the following operations:

Swap the integers written on vertices 111 and 222.
Pay one coin and change the integer written on vertex 222 to 777.
